title: ClassChangeRecipe
description:
Compares two categorical (classified) images, encodes per-pixel class transitions; emits a confidence band when both inputs carry per-class probability bands.

Use for: detecting land-cover transitions between two classification snapshots (forest→non-forest, cropland→urban). Output is a single integer per pixel encoding the (from-class, to-class) pair.

Pipeline: load `fromImage`/`toImage` (SEPAL classification recipe or EE asset) → look up class per pixel in each → encode `transition = fromIndex × len(toValues) + toIndex + 1` (1-based; indexes into sorted legend values) → if both have `probability_<value>` bands AND identical legend entry sets, compute `confidence` band; pixels below `options.minConfidence` are reassigned to whichever side's class is most probable.

AOI = intersection of `fromImage` and `toImage` geometries.

**Legend.** `legend.entries` is **GUI-auto-generated** from the cartesian product `fromImage.legendEntries × toImage.legendEntries`. User can edit labels/colors but not number/value. **LLM should leave `legend` absent** — GUI populates on first edit. EE backend doesn't read `legend`; transition encoding comes directly from each input's `legendEntries`.

dates.description: Cosmetic record of from/to dates the user is comparing. **EE backend does NOT read this** — date semantics come from underlying input classifications. Round-trip with GUI defaultModel only.

inputImage.title: ClassChangeInputImage
inputImage.description: Classified source image — SEPAL recipe (typically CLASSIFICATION) or EE Image/ImageCollection asset (whose bands may include per-class probabilities).

type.description: `RECIPE_REF` = SEPAL recipe id (typically CLASSIFICATION); `ASSET` = EE asset id.

id.description: Recipe id or EE asset id (per `type`).

band.description: Class-label band. Typically `'class'` for SEPAL classification recipes.

bands.description: Per-band visualization metadata keyed by band name. The class band's entry must carry `{values, labels, palette}` (mirroring the source's categorical viz) — EE backend uses these to detect `probability_<value>` bands for the optional confidence calc. Build via `image_bands({type, id})` for the names + `image_visualizations({type, id, band})` per band (returns `values`/`labels`/`palette` for categorical). Continuous bands map to `{}`.

legendEntries.description: Source classification legend `(value, label, color)`, mirroring source's `legend.entries`. Build via `image_visualizations({type, id, band})` for the class band — zip the returned `values`/`labels`/`palette` arrays into entries `{value, label, color}` (one per index). Hard cap 10 entries — cartesian product `len(from) × len(to)` else produces too many transition classes to label.

metadata.description: Optional. Full EE asset metadata blob (band specs, properties). For ASSET inputs use `asset_metadata({id})`; not applicable to RECIPE_REF. GUI refetches if absent.

visualizations.description: Optional. Source's preset visualizations. Use `image_visualizations({type, id})` (no `band` filter) to populate; GUI refetches on form open if absent.

legend.title: ClassChangeLegend
legend.description: Display legend for `transition` band. **GUI-auto-generated** from `fromImage.legendEntries × toImage.legendEntries`. **LLM should leave absent** when creating — GUI populates on first edit. EE backend doesn't read `legend.entries`; transition encoding derives from each input's `legendEntries`.

entries.description: One entry per (fromClass, toClass) pair. Values 1-based, encoded `fromIndex × len(toValues) + toIndex + 1`. Labels `"<fromLabel> -> <toLabel>"`.

options.title: ClassChangeOptions
options.description:
Confidence thresholding. **Only meaningful when both inputs have `probability_<value>` bands AND identical legend entry sets** — otherwise no `confidence` band is produced and these options are ignored.

(Note: legacy GUI `defaultModel.options` had stale `cloudThreshold`/`shadowThreshold`; not read by EE; rejected here per schema-as-truth.)

minConfidence.description:
Min combined per-pixel confidence (`mean(toCertainty, fromCertainty)` where each certainty = probability of assigned class minus probability of other side's class) to keep a transition. Below threshold, pixel reassigned to whichever side's class is more probable (suppresses low-confidence transitions).

Default 0 (no thresholding). Range 0–100. Slider disabled when probability bands not available on both inputs.
